directive.title: RFQ detail page loading skeleton and error boundary

auditor.summary: The build implements the skeleton component correctly with all four layout regions and proper data-testid attributes. However, the ErrorBoundary integration is fundamentally broken - the error card component is defined but never rendered by the ErrorBoundary, the retry mechanism uses window.location.reload() instead of router.refresh(), and the page queries the database directly instead of making API calls (making the Playwright tests ineffective). Additionally, the RFQDetailWithErrorBoundary and RFQErrorCard components use React hooks without 'use client' directives, which will cause Next.js 14 build failures. The build requires significant rework to meet the acceptance criteria.

top_issues.description: ErrorBoundary integration is broken. The ErrorBoundary component from components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx does not accept an errorFallback prop - it only accepts children and fallbackTitle. The RFQErrorCard component is defined but never rendered by the ErrorBoundary. The ErrorBoundary's built-in error UI does not contain data-testid='rfq-retry-cta', so the Playwright test for retry button visibility will fail.

Retry mechanism uses window.location.reload() instead of router.refresh() as specified in the acceptance criteria. This causes a full page reload instead of a soft re-fetch, which is worse UX and may lose state.

The ErrorBoundary is imported from '../../../components/ui/ErrorBoundary' but the build spec explicitly states 'Import the existing error boundary from lib/error-boundary (AUTO-014)'. This file does not exist in the repo ground truth - there is no lib/error-boundary file listed. This is a spec violation.
